persona.name: Andrés Morales

occupation.title: Kinesiology PhD Candidate
occupation.organization: UCLA Department of Kinesiology, Los Angeles, CA
occupation.description: Researches running economy and ground contact time optimization in endurance athletes. TAing undergraduate biomechanics courses while completing dissertation research on footwear-mediated changes in running mechanics. Lives on a graduate stipend — comfortable enough but carefully managed. Treats every major running shoe release as a professional development event.

persona.education: B.S. Kinesiology, UC Davis; currently pursuing Ph.D. in Exercise Science at UCLA
long_term_goals: Complete his dissertation and publish 2–3 papers in peer-reviewed running science journals
Run a sub-2:30 marathon before finishing his PhD
Become a professor who bridges academic running science and practical coaching
Inspire more Latino men to see academic and athletic achievement as compatible
persona.style: Enthusiastic and warm, leads with a story or personal anecdote before making a technical point. Mixes fluent academic vocabulary with casual SoCal conversation without self-consciousness. Physically confident — carries himself like an athlete even in lecture halls. Wears branded athletic gear from head to toe, color-coordinated. Easily the most animated person in a room and fully comfortable being so.

traits: Deeply curious — wants to understand mechanism, not just outcome
Outwardly optimistic with a competitive interior he doesn't always show
Community-oriented; believes in lifting people around him
Occasionally overcommits to projects and runs because he can't say no

interests: Trail and road running performance research
Shoe biomechanics and foam technology from a scientific lens
Pickup soccer with grad school friends
Music festivals and LA street food culture
Sneaker culture — follows releases but evaluates through a biomechanics filter
Mentoring undergrad athletes interested in research
likes: Trail-specific shoe tech (Hoka Speedgoat, Salomon Sense Ride)
Studying gait differences between shoe conditions in his own lab data
Post-run agua fresca from the Silver Lake farmers' market
Running with a big crew — the more chaotic, the better
Race-day playlist curation (always includes his dad's norteño playlist as a section)
Reading shoe company patents for fun
dislikes: Marketing copy that misappropriates biomechanics terminology
'Science-backed' claims that cite in-house studies with N=8
Overpriced lifestyle shoes that underdeliver on actual running performance
Humidity — misses how dry LA gets on hot Santa Ana days
Impostor syndrome flares during conference season

beliefs: The body is a research project you live in — and deserve to understand deeply
Community builds better runners than solitude, always
Academic credibility and authentic cultural identity are not in conflict
Good equipment matters, but training adaptation dwarfs any footwear effect
skills: Running biomechanics research design and force plate analysis
Coaching fundamentals adapted from his academic training
Community organizing — has built a 40-person grad student running group
Bilingual presentation (English and Spanish) for community science outreach

general: Reads every major shoe study that comes across his ResearchGate feed before forming opinions
Tests shoes on lab treadmill with his own instrumentation before recommending them to lab members
Organizes informal group runs for the UCLA running community every Tuesday and Thursday
Follows shoe releases with the same attention he gives peer-reviewed literature

morning: 8-mile run before lab (Tue/Thu/Sat/Sun), incorporating tempo segments on Thursdays
Breakfast burrito or fruit stand grab near campus
workday: Data analysis and dissertation writing in the morning lab block
Undergraduate TA sessions in early afternoon
Lab equipment setup for afternoon research sessions
evening: Pickup soccer or casual group run with grad friends
Cooks simple Mexican-American meals — arroz con pollo, canned bean tacos
Reads new shoe studies or industry news before bed
weekend: Long trail run in the San Gabriels on Saturdays (18–24 miles with 3,000+ feet gain)
Recovery brunch in Silver Lake Sunday, usually 8+ people
Calls his family in Fresno Sunday afternoons

health.physical: Exceptional aerobic fitness from high training volume and a lifetime of athletic activity. Manages periodic shin splint flares from overtraining — a recognized occupational hazard of being a running researcher who also runs too much. BMI at the low end; lean muscle mass from trail climbing.
health.mental: Mostly energized and positive; deeply motivated by his research and community. Occasional graduate school pressure anxiety, especially around publication timelines. Running is both his research subject and his primary mental reset.

relationships.name: Dr. Carmen Ortiz
relationships.description: Demanding mentor who pushes Andrés toward rigor. They have a productive tension — she keeps him focused, he brings infectious energy to the lab.

relationships.name: Felipe Morales
relationships.description: Works in construction in Phoenix. They ran their first half marathon together four years ago — Felipe did it in cargo shorts and still beat his seed time.

relationships.name: Sofia
relationships.description: Fellow PhD candidate studying nutritional periodization. They pace each other on Tuesday tempo runs and debate shoe company research ethics with equal intensity.
